evals.prompt: Roll my NVDA 155/140 bull put spread. I received $1.98 credit, expires May 1 2026.
evals.expected_output: Roll analysis showing the close cost for the current 155/140 spread, followed by ranked roll candidates (calendar, defensive diagonal, aggressive diagonal) at future expiries. Should include net roll credit/debit for each, new breakevens, PoP, and a recommendation based on roll quality.

assertions.name: shows_close_cost
assertions.description: Response shows the current cost to close the 155/140 spread (net debit to close)

assertions.name: has_roll_candidates
assertions.description: Response shows at least 2 roll candidates with different expiries or roll types (calendar, diagonal)

assertions.name: has_net_roll_credit
assertions.description: Each roll candidate shows the net roll credit or debit (new credit minus close cost)

assertions.name: has_new_metrics
assertions.description: Roll candidates include new breakeven, probability of profit, and return on risk

assertions.name: has_recommendation
assertions.description: Response includes a recommendation on which roll is best or whether to close instead

assertions.name: comparison_format
assertions.description: Response presents candidates in a comparison format (table or card) for easy decision-making

evals.prompt: I need to roll the put side of my AAPL iron condor. Strikes are 220/200 puts and 275/300 calls. Total credit was $2.86, expiry May 1 2026.
evals.expected_output: Roll analysis for just the put side (220/200) of the iron condor. Should show close cost for the put spread, roll candidates for the put side only, and note that the call side (275/300) stays open. Should flag that rolling one side makes the condor asymmetric.

assertions.name: rolls_put_side_only
assertions.description: Response correctly identifies that only the put side (220/200) is being rolled, not the call side

assertions.name: shows_close_cost
assertions.description: Response shows the cost to close the put spread (220/200)

assertions.name: has_roll_candidates
assertions.description: Response shows roll candidates for the put side at future expiries

assertions.name: notes_asymmetry
assertions.description: Response mentions that rolling one side makes the iron condor asymmetric or changes the risk profile

assertions.name: has_recommendation
assertions.description: Response includes guidance on best roll option or whether to close the whole condor

evals.prompt: My SPY 550/530 put spread is in the danger zone. Credit was $2.10, expires April 17 2026. What can I do?
evals.expected_output: Should trigger the roller skill since the user has a spread in danger and is asking what to do. Show roll options as a way to manage the position. Should also mention closing as an alternative. Should convey urgency given the danger zone context.

assertions.name: triggers_roller
assertions.description: Response uses the spread roller to find roll targets rather than just giving generic advice

assertions.name: shows_close_option
assertions.description: Response presents closing the position as one option alongside rolling

assertions.name: has_roll_candidates
assertions.description: Response shows concrete roll candidates with strikes, expiries, and credits

assertions.name: conveys_urgency
assertions.description: Response acknowledges the danger zone status and conveys appropriate urgency

assertions.name: has_recommendation
assertions.description: Response gives a clear recommendation (close, roll, or specific roll candidate)
